documentedClaim.statement: Merge by server ID following config precedence, then apply managed allowlists and trust.

entries.reason: Both the user-level ($CODEX_HOME/config.toml) and project-level (.codex/config.toml) sources define an mcp_servers table entry with the same ID 'shared-tools' but different command/args, a genuine collision. 'Merge by server ID' does not by itself say whether the two definitions deep-merge field by field, or the higher-precedence one fully replaces the other, or the collision is rejected outright with --strict-config-like behavior. No safe non-interactive way exists to observe Codex's actual resolved server definition for this ID without a real session, so selectedSource is UNKNOWN rather than a guessed winner (config precedence order itself -- CLI > nearest project > ancestor project > profile > user > system > default, per the same ontology row's Settings entry -- is a documented claim about config *values* generally, not confirmed here as applying identically to whole embedded mcp_servers tables).

documentedClaim.statement: Merge by server ID following config precedence, then apply managed allowlists and trust.

entries.reason: Only one source defines this ID, so there is no winner to guess between competing definitions -- if the documented 'merge by ID' claim holds, this entry simply survives untouched. What remains unconfirmed by this pass is the more basic question of whether Codex 0.144.5 really performs a per-ID merge at all, as opposed to (hypothetically) one config file's mcp_servers table wholesale replacing the other's; this pass has not behaviorally distinguished those two possibilities, so confirmed stays false even though selectedSource names a concrete path rather than UNKNOWN.
